why: resolve-governances binds artifact-type skill to the ACED squad, but ACED recuses on the SDD self-spec (boolean process-Gherkin, not ACED @rubric/@trigger agent-config suites; precedent PR #130 / #191). Re-resolved that unit's chain to the SDD defaults (sdd-spec-judge / sdd-impl-judge, sdd:*-governance bars); other artifact-types (suite/spec/code) already resolved SDD-default. Recorded, not a halt.

Cold spec-judge round 1 ALIGNED false (architect FAIL). The drafted mission-graph barrier fence let a barrier fence its own un-retired RAW predecessor: predecessor never starts, barrier never RAW-satisfied, project wedges permanently. Aggravated by the fence being fold-computed rather than a stored edge, so the graph stays acyclic and cycles reports the wedge healthy. Fix attempted: pin the fence predicate to un-retired plus a per-barrier predecessor-closure carve-out.

Cold spec-judge round 1 also FAILed builder: the new formation scenario a-project-wide-structural-act-escalates carried Given 'preserves every scenario verbatim', colliding with the frozen a-coverage-preserving-split-self-clears -- one input, two opposite Thens, invisible to the title-keyed addOnly diff. Resolved WITHOUT a Clearance floor: a split is node-scoped by definition in main's own prose, so the Givens were disjoint in fact but only implicitly; pinned the disjointness in the suite text (rather than one node) plus a scenario asserting a single-node split still self-clears. No frozen scenario edited. Judge round 2 independently verified no narrowing.

Cold spec-judge round 2 ALIGNED false: the round-1 carve-out was scoped per-barrier and did not compose. Two barriers in one project returned opposite verdicts on one snapshot (a barrier's exempt predecessor was still fenced by the OTHER barrier), violating the frozen fold-is-deterministic scenario, and wedged the project if the fence won. Fix attempted: make the exemption project-global (any un-retired barrier's RAW-predecessor closure).

Cold spec-judge round 3 ALIGNED false: the project-global exemption closed the closure hole but left the barrier-vs-barrier hole open -- barriers ARE Missions, so a barrier binds the holds-back-the-other-missions Given. Judge proved NO fold rule satisfies the block's five barrier scenarios, and derived the closed-form rule R'' with an induction proof of termination. Iteration cap (3) reached without convergence.

Cap-hit presented to the owner rather than auto-accepted, with the trajectory measured: scenario count 10->14->17, contradictions 1->1->3, and rounds 2-3 each refuted the scenarios added to fix the round before -- diverging, not converging. Root cause diagnosed as a METHOD error: the fence was specified by example (17 independent assertions, no stated fold rule), and pairwise Given overlap grows quadratically, so each added scenario created new chances to contradict. Owner directive: SPLIT. Ship the two clean halves (star blast-estimate node, formation barrier call-out); revert the mission-graph fence block entirely and re-file it as issue #224 to be designed RULE-FIRST from R'' + its termination proof + the three counterexample snapshots S1/S2/S3. Op5 lands partially and honestly; the fence is the only diverging half and nothing consumes it yet.
